description: Loop residual under flux-ratio proxy vs parity-signed model magnification vs model convergence, using GLAFIC v3 values (Kelly+2023). Tests whether the headline sign survives the mu->kappa substitution the referee requires.
verdict: Inv-kappa residual (+2.6 d) shares the sign of the flux-proxy prediction (+14.5 d) and the observation (+30.0 d); the sign evidence survives substitution of the 1/kappa potential proxy (amplitude differs).

provenance.primary_reference: Kelly et al. 2023, ApJ 948, 93 (arXiv:2305.06377), lensing-parameters table (GLAFIC v3)
provenance.transcription_note: Values transcribed via automated web extraction (ar5iv HTML of arXiv:2305.06377), corroborated by (i) two independent extractions returning identical kappa, (ii) internal consistency of (kappa,gamma,mu,parity) under mu = 1/[(1-kappa)^2 - gamma^2], and (iii) the SX/S1 magnification ratio 4.2/14.6 = 0.29 matching the Kelly+2023 measured value 0.30. VERIFY against the published table / archived GLAFIC maps before use in a final publication.
provenance.verification_status: web-extracted; pending direct confirmation against archived maps

caveats: 1/kappa is a proxy for potential depth under the isothermal assumption (beta_A=-1); the true TEP coupling variable is |Phi|, i.e. the lensing potential psi, which would be the cleanest tracer but is not tabulated in Kelly+2023.
Total kappa includes the cluster-member-galaxy contribution at S1-S4; a cluster-only potential decomposition could shift the comparison and should be checked.
GLAFIC v3 values are web-transcribed (see provenance); confirm against archived maps before publication.
